result: If I could change the world in one way, I would dissolve the illusion of separation—the ingrained belief that we are isolated selves, fundamentally disconnected from other beings, from nature, and from the cosmos itself. This single shift would unravel the roots of greed, exploitation, loneliness, and violence, because the pain we inflict on what we perceive as "other" would be felt as clearly as our own. It wouldn’t erase difference or conflict overnight, but it would make authentic empathy the baseline of existence, not an effortful virtue. Every decision—from how we feed ourselves to how we structure societies—would start from a place of shared aliveness, and that feels like the only change deep enough to heal everything else.
